assertion_results.text: The plan references checking out TC-9005 (the Target Branch) before creating the task branch — includes git checkout TC-9005 or equivalent, not git checkout main (constraint 3.1, Step 5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Branch Operations' section shows Step 1: 'git checkout TC-9005' followed by 'git pull', then Step 2: 'git checkout -b TC-9205'. The plan states: 'This checks out the feature branch TC-9005 as the base for the new task branch, per the Target Branch section in the task description.'

assertion_results.text: The plan specifies opening a PR with --base TC-9005 targeting the feature branch, not --base main (Step 10)
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Push and PR' section includes: 'gh pr create --base TC-9005 --title ...' The plan explicitly states: 'The PR targets TC-9005 (--base TC-9005), the feature branch, not main. This follows constraint 3.3: "gh pr create MUST always specify --base <target-branch> matching the task's Target Branch value."'

assertion_results.text: The plan names the task branch TC-9205 (the task issue ID), not TC-9005 (the feature branch) — the task branch is distinct from the Target Branch (constraint 3.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan shows: 'git checkout -b TC-9205' and states: 'The task branch is named after the task issue ID TC-9205 (not TC-9005, which is the feature branch). This follows constraint 3.1.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 1 of: migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, migration/src/lib.rs (constraint 1.5, Steps 4-9 unaffected by Target Branch)
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Code Inspection' section lists all 3 files: (1) 'Read migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' to understand the migration pattern, (2) 'Read entity/src/advisory.rs' to verify the status column is no longer referenced, (3) 'Read migration/src/lib.rs' to understand migration registration.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit command includes: 'git commit --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'' and the Key commit conventions section states: '--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' included (constraint 2.3)'.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9205 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit message is 'feat(migration): add migration to drop advisory status column' which follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'migration'. The footer contains 'Implements TC-9205'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying TC-9005 as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Target Branch Analysis' section states: 'The task description contains a Target Branch section with value TC-9005. This is a feature branch (not main), indicating this task is part of a feature-branch workflow.' It also notes: 'All branch operations must account for this: the task branch is created from TC-9005, and the PR targets TC-9005.'
